name: Baton Rouge (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field; the 'red stick' for which Baton Rouge is named

description: A red field charged at the centre with the seal of the City of Baton Rouge.
history: Used by the City of Baton Rouge, the capital of Louisiana, named in 1699 by French explorer Pierre Le Moyne d'Iberville after a red ceremonial pole marking a tribal boundary.

features.symbolism: A red field, recalling the city's name — Baton Rouge means 'red stick' in French, after a red-stained cypress pole reported by early French explorers as marking the boundary between two indigenous nations.

features.role: Seal of the City of Baton Rouge
features.symbolism: The municipal seal, featuring an upright red stick (le baton rouge) — the name given to the location by French explorer Pierre Le Moyne d'Iberville in 1699, after a red-stained pole he saw marking the boundary between Houma and Bayougoula tribal hunting grounds.
